title.zh: 大腦「煞車失靈」解密：前額葉異常放電如何驅動強迫行為
title.en: The Brain's Broken Brake: How Frontal Lobe Gamma Waves Fuel OCD Symptoms

summary.zh:
一項刊登於《Cell》期刊的突破性研究，首次捕捉到強迫症(OCD)患者大腦前額葉皮質的異常神經活動模式。科學家透過顱內電極監測發現，當患者強迫症狀加劇時，大腦內側眶額葉皮質(OFC)的低伽瑪波活動會顯著升高，就像『卡住的唱片』不斷重複播放錯誤訊號。

研究團隊設計症狀誘發實驗，同步記錄16名OCD患者的神經電活動。當患者報告強迫思維或行為增強時，前側內眶額葉區域的伽瑪波(30-50Hz)功率平均提升37%。更關鍵的是，當對腹側基底核進行深部腦刺激(DBS)治療時，不僅症狀緩解，該區域的異常伽瑪波也隨之下降。這項發現為『大腦煞車系統失靈』的OCD理論提供了直接證據，未來可望發展出精準調控異常腦波的個人化療法。
summary.en:
A landmark study in Cell reveals the neural signature of obsessive-compulsive disorder with unprecedented precision. Using intracranial electrodes in 16 OCD patients, researchers discovered that spikes in low-gamma (30-50Hz) power within the anteromedial orbitofrontal cortex (OFC) consistently correlate with worsening symptoms during provocation tasks—like a neurological fingerprint of compulsive urges.

The team demonstrated a direct therapeutic link: when deep brain stimulation (DBS) targeted the ventral basal ganglia, both symptom relief and OFC gamma power reduction occurred simultaneously. This dual effect suggests that pathological gamma oscillations may drive OCD's characteristic 'stuck' thought patterns. The findings open new avenues for developing closed-loop neuromodulation devices that could detect and interrupt harmful brain activity in real time, potentially revolutionizing treatment for the 2-3% of people affected by this debilitating condition.

keyArticle.title: Human orbitofrontal neural activity is linked to obsessive-compulsive behavioral dynamics
